name: Morroccan Spiced Olives
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the olives of any brine.
2. Slowly heat olive oil over low heat. Remove from heat and add garlic, rosemary, chile-garlic paste, and vinegar.
3. Pour marinade over olives and let stand at least 2 hours (the flavor gets hotter the longer it stands).
- Optionally, you can refrigerate the mixture for up to a week.
Variation:
- Mediterranean-inspired hors d'oeuvre
- 4 ounces Montrachet or Leazay goat cheese
- 1 to 2 cloves garlic, chopped
- 1 tsp finely chopped cilantro (optional)
- 1 tsp medium salsa (or to taste)
- 1/2 tsp thyme
- 1 tsp olive oil
- Approximately 1/2 a French baguette, cut into 1/2 to 3/4 inch slices
Serve the spiced olives with the prepared goat cheese mixture on bread slices. Enjoy with a glass of medium-bodied Cabernet Sauvignon with noticeable character, such as those from California, Chile, or Spain.
